1989_09_03-India-Mumbai-ISKCON_Chowpatty-BG_3_9-Devotional-Service-makes-one-Efficient-and-Responsible-st2-vr01

Radhanath Swami Transcriptions Template

Meta Data v8

Event Name/Title

Devotional service, in addition to purifying material desires, also makes one the most responsible, highly efficient benefactor of humanity

Event Date

03/09/1989

Event Location

India, Mumbai, ISKCON Chowpatty

Event Type

Temple - BG class

Transcriber

Vinaya Nimai Das

Completed

08/05/2021

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-042-Parents%20Don't%20Like%20Their%20Children%20To%20Mix%20With%20Sadhus%20(Mumbai)-BG_3_9-dt_89_09_03.mp3

Source Video

N.A.

Verse

Bg. 3.9

Tags – Places

Alakapuri, Kurukshetra, Earth

Tags – Pastimes

Dhruva Maharaj performs austere devotional service in the forest, Dhruva Maharaj fights the Yakshas, Lord Krishna’s plans for the Pandavas

Tags – Persons

Dhruva Maharaj, Suruchi, Suniti, Uttanapada, Uttama, Svayambhuva Manu, Arjuna, Krishna, Dhritarashtra, Duryodhana

Tags – Themes

Service, Responsibility, Sincerity, Purification of material desires, Anger utilized in service of God, Holy names dispel illusion, Devotee an efficient and beneficial worker, Attachment transforms to lust, Scriptures must be understood from advanced souls, Mode of goodness also causes bondage, A devotee is a genuine well-wisher of his family members

yajṣārthāt karmaṇo ’nyatra
loko ’yaṁ karma-bandhanaḥ
tad-arthaṁ karma kaunteya
mukta-saṅgaḥ samācara

[Bg. 3.9]

[Work done as a sacrifice for Viṣṇu has to be performed; otherwise work causes bondage in this material world. Therefore, O son of Kuntī, perform your prescribed duties for His satisfaction, and in that way you will always remain free from bondage.]

Purport

Since one has to work even for the simple maintenance of the body, the prescribed duties for a particular social position and quality are so made that that purpose can be fulfilled. Yajṣa means Lord Viṣṇu, or sacrificial performances. All sacrificial performances also are meant for the satisfaction of Lord Viṣṇu. The Vedas enjoin: yajṣo vai viṣṇuḥ. In other words, the same purpose is served whether one performs prescribed yajṣas or directly serves Lord Viṣṇu. Kṛṣṇa consciousness is therefore performance of yajṣa as it is prescribed in this verse. The varṇāśrama institution also aims at satisfying Lord Viṣṇu. Varṇāśramācāravatā puruṣeṇa paraḥ pumān/ viṣṇur ārādhyate (Viṣṇu Purāṇa 3.8.8).

Therefore one has to work for the satisfaction of Viṣṇu. Any other work done in this material world will be a cause of bondage, for both good and evil work have their reactions, and any reaction binds the performer. Therefore, one has to work in Kṛṣṇa consciousness to satisfy Kṛṣṇa (or Viṣṇu); and while performing such activities one is in a liberated stage. This is the great art of doing work, and in the beginning this process requires very expert guidance. One should therefore act very diligently, under the expert guidance of a devotee of Lord Kṛṣṇa, or under the direct instruction of Lord Kṛṣṇa Himself (under whom Arjuna had the opportunity to work). Nothing should be performed for sense gratification, but everything should be done for the satisfaction of Kṛṣṇa. This practice will not only save one from the reaction of work, but also gradually elevate one to transcendental loving service of the Lord, which alone can raise one to the kingdom of God.

Lecture Transcription

oṁ namo bhagavate vāsudevāya
janmādy asya yato ’nvayād itarataś cārtheṣv abhijṣaḥ svarāṭ
tene brahma hṛdā ya ādi-kavaye muhyanti yat sūrayaḥ
tejo-vāri-mṛdāṁ yathā vinimayo yatra tri-sargo ’mṛṣā
dhāmnā svena sadā nirasta-kuhakaṁ satyaṁ paraṁ dhīmah

[SB 1.1.1]

dharmaḥ projjhita-kaitavo ’tra paramo nirmatsarāṇāṁ satāṁ
vedyaṁ vāstavam atra vastu śivadaṁ tāpa-trayonmūlanam
śrīmad-bhāgavate mahā-muni-kṛte kiṁ vā parair īśvaraḥ
sadyo hṛdy avarudhyate ’tra kṛtibhiḥ śuśrūṣubhis tat-kṣaṇāt

[SB 1.1.2]

nārāyaṇaṁ namaskṛtya
naraṁ caiva narottamam
devīṁ sarasvatīṁ vyāsaṁ
tato jayam udīrayet

[SB 1.2.3]

munayaḥ sādhu pṛṣṭo ’haṁ
bhavadbhir loka-maṅgalam
yat kṛtaḥ kṛṣṇa-sampraśno
yenātmā suprasīdati

[SB 1.2.4]

[mangalacarana]

hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare/hare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare

In the Bhagavad-gita, third chapter the Supreme Lord Shri Krishna instructs us:

yajṣārthāt karmaṇo ’nyatra
loko ’yaṁ karma-bandhanaḥ
tad-arthaṁ karma kaunteya
mukta-saṅgaḥ samācara

[Bg. 3.9]

Pious action by itself can not replace the need for spiritual practice and saintly association

Work done as sacrifice for the Supreme Lord Vishnu has to be performed, otherwise any work you do for any other purpose whether it be in the mode of goodness, pious or ignorance is only a cause of bondage.

The Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Shri Krishna; he is describing in Bhagavad-gita, the method by which we could live within this world without ever being entangled by the sources of misery which are due to bondage to the threefold miseries of material existence. There are actions in the mode of goodness, actions in the mode of passion, and actions in the mode of ignorance. Sometimes, most often, people's conception of religion is piety: by doing good to others, by not causing any harm to others, by giving in charity... and through this process really whether there is a God or is not a God doesn't really make any difference, because I'm doing good and there's really no reason to engage in sadhana – spiritual practice, there's no need to associate with saintly persons, because after all we are doing our duty honestly and we are not harming anyone. And, throughout the world especially in India this is the conception of religion but in Bhagavad-gita which is the basic standard textbook describing the real meaning and purpose of religion, this concept is considered only for those who are less intelligent. Krishna describes in Gita, that pious action, although it is in the mode of goodness and it will lead to fruitful prosperity in this world, it is the source also of bondage and suffering. Just as impious actions are the cause of suffering and bondage, but to a greater and more immediate degree. Therefore, Krishna says that work done as a sacrifice of devotion to the supreme, that work alone opens the doors to liberation and any other action for any other purpose is the cause of bondage. So, Krishna is teaching us in Bhagavad-gita how we can be practical people of this world working within our careers and at the same time be progressing forward, toward the perfection of yoga. Some people think that when you become spiritually minded; when you accept a guru then you become very impractical people in society: very non-functional, very careless and irresponsible toward one's duties. But we find in the scriptures that the most capable, qualified, responsible and beneficial persons within the history of this world were those not who were pious, but those who were surrendered their lives at the lotus feet of guru and Krishna and there's one very famous example which we will speak about this evening. You have all heard of Dhruva Maharaj.

Dhruva Maharaj after seeking his mother’s advice, leaves home for the forest

Dhruva Maharaj was a young child. Sometimes parents, they want to keep their children away from sadhus because they're afraid ‘If our children associate with sadhus, then they'll run away from home, they'll go off to some ashram, they won't take care of the family, they'll be irresponsible and where will that leave us?’ How many of you have thought like this? Raise your hand. Every one of you in the room have thought like this whether you raise your hand or not. This is the natural way of thinking due to illusory propaganda of this world: that if someone takes their religion too seriously, too seriously, then my God! we’ll lose that person that person will become a renunciate, he will be unfunctionable, he will become like a eunuch to the rest of society and my god! what will he do for the family? Huh? So, let us consider the case of Dhruva Maharaj. Dhruva Maharaj at the age of five, you know the story... was bitten by the serpent of the harsh words of his stepmother Suruci, when she condemned him that he will never be able to sit on the throne with his father because he was born of the womb of an inferior woman Suniti. So, Dhruva Maharaj being a child was so much hurt; his feelings were so hurt by his stepmother and even worse were they trampled upon by his father because his father said nothing to defend him, that with tears in his eyes he ran he went running to his mother: “Please Mataji! What am I supposed to do? You know what my father has done and my stepmother...” And his mother was a very honest and very religious woman. She said, “My dear son Dhruva, it pains my heart to tell you but what your stepmother said is true. Your father has no attraction for me. I have no prominence in this kingdom, I am an inferior woman with inferior qualities to my co-wife and therefore the fact is you are so unfortunate to have taken birth in my womb that you will never be given the position that you desire in your father's life.” Dhruva Maharaj, his eyes full of tears and his mother with eyes full of tears, they began to look at one another and Dhruva said, “Mataji I want to attain the kingdom greater than my father. What do I have to do?” And his mother, she said, “Dhruva, anything you desire can be fulfilled if you attain the blessings of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Shri Hari.” Dhruva said, “Where do I go to find Lord Shri Hari?” She said, “We have been told many stories that throughout the ages the great saints and sages, they have gone to the forest and there they have the darshan of Lord Hari.” So Dhruva Maharaj, a child who did not go through the educational system to educate himself how to live in this world, who had no means or knowledge of how to maintain and protect himself; he had such determination, such eagerness to find Krishna that he left his home and went to the forest.

Krishna reciprocates with sincerity of Dhruva by sending his pure devotee, Narada Muni.

Srila Rupa Gosvami describes in the Bhakti-rasamrita-sindhu that, the price of attaining the vision of Krishna, the cost of the mercy of Krishna is simply, the intense eagerness for Krishna, the intense longing, the intense enthusiasm, the intense eagerness that ‘I must have Krishna, at all costs I must have Krishna’. One must be greedy for Krishna just like a lusty man is greedy for a woman, eager for a woman; just like a greedy man, a miserly man is greedy, anxious, lusty for more and more money and power, we must become more eager for Krishna than that. And Dhruva Maharaj had that eagerness dridha-vrata: he made a firm determined vow that ‘I must attain devotion to Krishna.’ So, he went to the forest not considering his age, not considering the serpents, the lions, the tigers, the leopards, the mosquitoes, the dacoits, the cold nights, the hot days, not considering where he would get his food he went to the forest because he had such eagerness to find Krishna. And when one sincerely desires God, that God who is within the heart who knows our very essence of all thoughts and desires, he reciprocates. And how does he reciprocate? Nowhere do we find practically nowhere will Krishna ever reveal himself directly but when he sees someone is genuine and sincere, he sends to that person the ultimate blessing of the association of his pure devotees – the spiritual master. So, Krishna directed Narada Muni to that place to give Dhruva Maharaj the means and the ways to know God. So Narada Muni approached Dhruva; first he tested him, “Dhruva you're a young child, don't take the words of your father so seriously. Go home.” Dhruva Maharaj, after hearing the preaching of Narada, he said, “My dear sadhuji Narada, with all due respect, if you are not willing to show me the way back to Godhead then please get out of my way.” When Narada Muni saw Dhruva Maharaj's determination he said, “Ahn! The way to understand and realize God is very simple: first you must perform some tapasya and simultaneously you must always remember the holy names of the Lord.” And he initiated him in the mantra oṁ namo bhagavate vāsudevāya.

Devotional service purifies Dhruva Maharaj’s heart of all material desires

So Dhruva Maharaj after only a few months, was given the divine vision of Shri Vishnu, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. And Vishnu offered him all benedictions; he said, “Dhruva you want... you were troubled... you approached me because you wanted a kingdom greater than your father. Now I am willing to give you a kingdom greater than that of Lord Brahma.” And Dhruva Maharaj replied, “My Lord! All my material desires for approaching you, after being purified by the words of Narada Muni and purified by the beautiful vision of your divine form have been completely removed. At this time I only want one thing: that I always remember you and then may I always be the servant of your servants. Indeed I approached you for broken pieces of glass, but now I have attained the most valuable and rare diamond of your darshan. So, Lord Hari, he told Dhruva Maharaj that, “This benediction will be granted. But at the same time Dhruva, although you are so very content to live in this forest and meditate on my glories forever, as the son of a king it is your duty to go home, to go to your father and assist him in his duties to the world.” At that time Narada Muni during the penances and austerities of Dhruva Maharaj, Narada Muni went to his father Uttanapada, who was very much upset that due to his negligence, Dhruva had left home and was living in the forest and Narada Muni told him that, “Soon, your five year son will come back but he will not be the same boy you knew; he will be a paramahamsa, he will be a highly divinely realized soul who is supremely fixed in devotion to the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord and will always be hearing and chanting his glories. He will be a magnificent example of compassion and mercy, the lover of all living beings. Indeed! He will be the next king of the earth.

Dhruva Maharaj returns home to accept responsibilities as the king of the earth

So, Dhruva returned home and he accepted the responsibilities. And because of his highly realized knowledge of the absolute truth instead of enthroning his older brother Uttama as king, Uttanapada made Dhruva the king of the earth. He was a very responsible king: his duties to his family, his duty to his citizens were impeccable in how they were carried out. He was the protector of everyone, and anyone who misbehaved; he was like the rod of chastisement. He was fixed utterly in the divine will of God in his duties in this world and therefore he never incurred karma, he never incurred bondage because as Krishna says, “Work done as a sacrifice for Vishnu has to be performed!” Now crazy minded people think that if they dedicate themselves and surrender themselves to much to Krishna they'll be irresponsible in this world but raise your hand today if you consider yourself more responsible and more dedicated to your service and to your family than Dhruva Maharaj. If not then give up your nonsense and understand that the goal of life and the perfection of your duty is to surrender utterly to God and that is a fact. Don't let the demoniac propaganda of this world mislead you. Surrender to God is the ultimate goal of everyone's life, in whatever field of action you are in.

Dhruva Maharaj fights the Yakshas in order to punish them for killing his brother

Now one day Dhruva Maharaj came home and he found the news that his brother Uttama had been killed; he was killed by one of the Yakshas, which is a dynasty of rather low class warrior men. When Dhruva Maharaj heard this news he became very angry. He decided that the Yaksha dynasty, for the atrocity that they had committed, they must be destroyed. So, he mounted his chariot, not with armies but alone. He was utterly fearless and he rode to the city of Alakapuri which was where the Yakshas resided and he blew his conchshell and challenged them to battle. Now the Yakshas are famous throughout the world as very fierce warriors. Not only are they fierce warriors but they possess very highly powerful mystic siddhis, by which they can conquer almost anyone. A hundred and thirty thousand Yakshas came out to fight against Dhruva. Each one of them, a notable trained warrior and a mystic yogi. Dhruva stood alone against all of them, but Dhruva had one thing that they did not have: he had complete faith in the protection of Krishna. They had their abilities, they had their force of numbers, all he had was his devotion to Krishna because he knew it was his duty to fight with them.

Devotees of the Lord are more responsible than materialistic persons

Some people think that devotees of the Lord when they become sadhus, they become so peaceful that they really can't perform their duties properly. Sometimes we were constantly getting the question that if you become a devotee and you become so good-hearted to all others then what if people take advantage of you and try to cheat you and everything else. Then how can you do your business if you're not ready to fight and protect what is right? Dhruva was a sadhu have the highest order but my God when he saw injustice is committed by the Yakshas, he became angry like fire. It is not that a devotee cannot become angry.

Difference between the anger of materialistic person and the anger of a devotee

A sadhu becomes angry sometimes but his anger is in the service of God. His anger is not because of frustrated sense gratification like an ordinary man; an ordinary man it is said

dhyāyato viṣayān puṁsaḥ
saṅgas teṣūpajāyate
saṅgāt saṣjāyate kāmaḥ
kāmāt krodho ’bhijāyate

[Bg. 2.62]

[While contemplating the objects of the senses, a person develops attachment for them, and from such attachment lust develops, and from lust anger arises.]

krodhād bhavati sammohaḥ
sammohāt smṛti-vibhramaḥ
smṛti-bhraṁśād buddhi-nāśo
buddhi-nāśāt praṇaśyati

[Bg. 2.63]

[From anger, complete delusion arises, and from delusion bewilderment of memory. When memory is bewildered, intelligence is lost, and when intelligence is lost one falls down again into the material pool.]

‘That when contemplating the objects of the senses one develops attachment for them and from such attachment lust awakens and when lust is unfulfilled anger arises and when one becomes angry, one loses... one's memory is bewildered, then one's intelligence is lost, then one engages in abominable activities, offensive activities which he regrets later and thus he falls down.’

kāma eṣa krodha eṣa
rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥ
mahāśano mahā-pāpmā
viddhy enam iha vairiṇam

[Bg. 3.37]

[The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: It is lust only, Arjuna, which is born of contact with the material mode of passion and later transformed into wrath, and which is the all-devouring sinful enemy of this world.]

‘Krishna says it is lust only Arjuna which is later transformed into anger which is the all-devouring sinful enemy of this world.’

So, how is it a great sadhu like Dhruva was intensely angry like fire and he's worshiped as a great soul? Because his anger was simply a tool and instrument to facilitate his service to God. Therefore that anger is considered transcendental. And he began to fight, and he began to shoot three arrows at a time with such speed and such ferocity that the Yakshas’ limbs were being pierced by his arrows, and they were screaming in agony as Dhruva was piercing their bodies and they began to glorify his activities. But then the Yakshas, they began to shoot six arrows each at a time and from their arrows due to their mystic powers, they were shooting literally so many arrows; there were millions of arrows at a time flying toward Dhruva, plus they were throwing lances and swords and bludgeons and clubs and every other weapon imaginable. And there were so many of these dastardly weapons coming at Dhruva that nobody could even see Dhruva. He was completely engulfed like the sun being covered by a dark cloud and Dhruva disappeared. All that could be seen was the deadly weapons of the Yakshas all around him. At that time the great sages and the demigods, they were thinking Dhruva is finished, Dhruva is dead, but right at that moment Dhruva like the sun coming out from a dark cloud appeared and he was more ferocious than ever and he began to shoot his arrows and as he began to shoot he was killing all the Yakshas. There was a river of blood. There were heads floating in that river of blood and limbs and arms and legs and all the beautiful ornaments of the Yakshas, and soon there was only a few Yakshas remaining and they ran back to the city.

Sages advise Dhruva that the holy name of God dispels the darkness of illusion

But Dhruva was very intelligent; he understood ‘I have not won this war yet because these Yakshas are very great mystics: what is their next trick?’ and then by the mystic illusory potency of the Yakshas they created an illusion. First the sky became dark with clouds and there was very frightening thunder loud grumbling thunder [Maharaja makes sound of thunder] in all directions. Then in all directions there were lightning bolts flying, creating a very horrifying effect, and then immense volumes of rain began to shower from the clouds [Maharaja makes sound of rain shower]and then the rain transformed into blood, pus, urine, stool, bile; the most abominable substances. And then severed heads and limbs began to shower from the sky, and then swords and trident and clubs began to shower like rain by the trillions from the sky, and then lions and tigers and venomous snakes by the millions started falling from the clouds upon Dhruva Maharaj, and then fire in all directions started burning, and then the enormous sound of the ocean which takes place at the time of dissolution was heard on all four sides and the huge waves of the ocean of dissolution started coming towards Dhruva and in this condition Dhruva was bewildered. He considered himself defeated.

Seeing the situation the great sages who are the well-wishers of all human society they lined up before Dhruva Maharaj and they began to call out to him in his horrible predicament, “Dhruva Maharaj, don't you know that this is all due to the illusion of maya and that the name of God which is non different than God himself is like the shining sun which dispels all the darkness of illusion. Simply remember the holy name of Lord Hari and all dangers, all darkness, all miseries will immediately be removed. Dhruva do you not remember the mantra given to you by Narada Muni? Remember the name of God, that is all that is required in this world to remove to remove all dangers however severe they may be.” And hearing these words, Dhruva Maharaj called out the name of God and then he became fearless. He became divinely empowered and inspired by this transcendental sound vibration. And then he took a bow from his arrow, I mean he took an arrow from his quiver which was the arrow of Narayana, the Narayana-astra and he pulled us arrow on the string of his bow and from that one arrow came shooting out hundreds and thousands of millions of arrows which started to totally annihilate the Yakshas.

Svayambhuva Manu advises Dhruva Maharaj to stop fighting and avoid unnecessary violence

At that time the Yakshas were utterly defeated but Dhruva Maharaj in his spirit of chivalry was just killing them one after another after another. Then Svayambhuva Manu appeared, who was the grandfather of Dhruva. He said, “Dhruva Maharaj, you've already defeated the Yakshas. They are the descendants of Kuvera, the treasurer of the demigods. To kill them any more as there's no need. You have already taught them a good lesson, you have already defeated them. There is no need for unnecessary violence. So, please be merciful upon them.” Upon hearing these words Dhruva Maharaj, he became peaceful. At that time Kuvera the treasurer of the devatas appeared on the scene, because he was very pleased with Dhruva Maharaj. He said, “Dhruva you could have killed all these Yakshas, they deserve to be killed but because of your kind and generous heart as a pure devotee of the Lord, you have forgiven them therefore I am very pleased with you. And as the treasurer of the demigods I want to award you any benediction you desire. You can ask of me anything; all the wealth of all the demigods are at my disposal. All the opulence of the heavens, I can give you. What do you desire?” And Dhruva Maharaj with all humility, he offered his obeisances to Kuvera and he said, “The only desire I have in life is that my faith in the mercy of Krishna may ever increase and that I may always remember his lotus feet and I may always be engaged in the service of his devotees. Let that be my only desire in life.” And Kuvera granted him the blessing of that benediction and departed for his abode.

By surrendering in devotion one becomes the most highly efficient benefactor of humanity

So, we learned from this story how when one becomes a surrendered soul and makes devotion to God, the priority of his life, such a person becomes the most highly efficient benefactor of all humanity. Such a person becomes the most highly efficient and effective worker in whatever field of activity he may be in, becomes most responsible not only for the body but for the soul of all others. And how such a person who surrenders their life to God, becomes perfectly protected by the Lord and the devotees of the Lord at all times, and how by simply chanting the name of God we can attain this divine position of consciousness and by simply chanting the name of God we can be freed from all suffering and fear? Bhagavatam states

etan nirvidyamānānām
icchatām akuto-bhayam
yogināṁ nṛpa nirṇītaṁ
harer nāmānukīrtanam

[SB 2.1.11]

[O King, constant chanting of the holy name of the Lord after the ways of the great authorities is the doubtless and fearless way of success for all, including those who are free from all material desires, those who are desirous of all material enjoyment, and also those who are self-satisfied by dint of transcendental knowledge.]

‘That one who chants the name of the God with a sincere heart becomes fearless, all the miseries of material existence are removed from his life, in due course of time. And such a person attains the highest stage of peace tranquility and love.’

So, let us contemplate, meditate and take very seriously these words of Lord Shri Krishna from the third chapter of Gita and let these words be confirmed by the lives of the great souls such as Dhruva Maharaj. Let our life be perfected, by making our priority in life, the purification of the heart by the chanting of the name of God. And let our every action, our every word and our every thought be an act of devotion to the Lord, and in this condition of life we should have faith, we should have complete faith that Krishna will protect us! Our family, our friends, all of our responsibilities they are no longer simply dependent on our limited abilities in this world which are very finite and temporary. But not only ourselves, but all those who are near and dear to us, when we surrender to God, they are all protected, maintained by the loving hands of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. That is the goal of perfection of life and it is available for anyone who sincerely chants the holy names. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna!

Is there any questions?

Q&A Transcription

The disease of attachment when further aggravated transforms into lust

[devotee:] You said that in third chapter of Bhagavad-gita it is said that by concentrating the object of senses, attachment….

[Maharaja:] That is the second chapter.

[devotee:] and lust and kama, krodha, lobha, moha, mada, matsara. The point is today morning, Mahabharata we saw: the total cause of trouble was not lust but attachment. Attachment because the second time he was asked Duryodhana compelled Dhritarashtra that you have to give me permission otherwise I'll commit suicide, that we have to play again, gambling match. Then Dhritarashtra was forced… it is not lust but attachment actually. Whether attachment is more dangerous or lust is more dangerous?

[Maharaja:] Hmm! While contemplating the objects of the senses one develops attachment. From attachment lust develops – they’re inseparable. When there is too much attachment, lust is the inevitable fruit of that attachment. Without attachment lust cannot exist. Lust is born of attachment. Hmm? Lust is the product of attachment. It's like asking which is more dangerous, the fire or the heat of the fire? Huh? Which is more dangerous? Can you separate the two? You cannot have heat, you cannot have fire without heat and heat cannot exist without fire. Okay? So, attachment and lust are simply different stages of the same disease. Attachment is the initial stage and when that state... when that disease becomes further aggravated, it transforms into lust. And when it becomes further aggravated it becomes anger, and then illusion, and then bewilderment of memory, and then loss of intelligence and then one falls down; one’s spiritual life dies. They’re just different gradations of stages of the same disease of material attachment. Therefore, if you are detached then there's no question of lust or anger or envy or any of these bad qualities. And the fact is you cannot become detached because your nature is to find pleasure. So, you have to find attachment to something. Vishvamitra Muni was detached, but as soon as the heavenly damsel Menaka came before him, he became attached because the soul is by nature pleasure seeking. So, real detachment from the things of this world which provoke lust and anger is, you have to become attached to God. To the degree you become attached to Krishna, you become detached from material existence. Hmm? What you're saying is true; attachment is the root cause of lust and all other bad qualities, but attachment to Krishna is the cure for the disease of material attachment. paraṁ dṛṣṭvā nivartate [Bg. 2.59]. When one experiences the higher taste, the higher pleasure, of attachment to the, to hearing and chanting the glories of the Lord then all the diseases of material attachment are removed permanently from the heart.

[Maharaja:] Here's a question.

One must understand the scripture through the vision of great souls

[devotee:] From today’s Mahabharata itself...

[Maharaja:] I didn't see it and even if I did, I don't understand Hindi. So, you'll have to tell me what you want me to…

[devotee:] We have seen it that, Dhritarashtra was playing game with Yudhishthira. He had given the instruction to play it once again. Now Yudhishthira was knowing it that all the Kauravas were playing the game for ruining the whole family. And it was completely impractical also, yet he has accepted it. On the other hand we see Krishna when talking to Arjuna, then he instructs us that whenever there is something which is impractical we have to fight it out or you have to obstruct it. Now we cannot compare it. Yudhishthira is right on one side or Krishna is right on other side.

[Maharaja:] The fact of the matter is: if you want to understand Mahabharata or any scripture, you have to understand it through the words of the advanced souls who understand why everything is happening. Otherwise you will be bewildered; you cannot understand. The shastras, according to the shastras, tad-vijṣānārthaṁ sa gurum evābhigacchet [Muṇḍaka Upaniṣad 1.2.12] They, the scriptures cannot be properly understood unless you hear the commentary of the great souls because they know the will of God. If you try to understand the scriptures on your own you will be endlessly bewildered: ‘why is this happening, why is that happening’. So, the fact is Yudhishthira was simply an instrument in the hands of Krishna. Yudhishthira was under the influence of yogamaya. The fact is Krishna wanted Yudhishthira to gamble in that match and Krishna wanted Yudhishthira to lose everything. Because Krishna wanted... he had a divine plan of glorifying the Pandavas by putting them in very very dangerous predicaments, to show how his devotees never give up devotion to the Lord under any situation. How, whether a devotee has everything or nothing, he remains faithful to the mercy of God. Han? And Krishna also [00:44:24 – 00:44:34 pause] wanted to create a situation where the Bhagavad-gita could be spoken.

Krishna fulfills the desires of demoniac persons but temporarily

And at the same time, Krishna fulfills the desires of everyone. He also wanted Duryodhana and Dhritarashtra to have what they wanted – for some time. They had such intense greed for the kingdom and such intense greed to cheat God's devotees that Krishna said ‘All right! Then you cheat them: I will give you the facility to cheat them, I will give you the facility to fulfill all your demoniac desires, if that's what you really want!” How do you think the demons of this world are so powerful? Krishna's allowing them to fulfill their desires. How do you think Hiranyakashipu was allowed to persecute Prahlad in such a way? Krishna could have stopped him immediately. Even when Lord Narahari started fighting with Hiranyakashipu, he could have killed him instantly but when Lord Narasimhadev was fighting with Hiranyakashipu Lord Narasimhadev appeared for some time to be losing the battle. Hiranyakashipu actually thought he was going to win and Narasimhadev was giving him that, he was playing with him. Just like a cat plays with a rat. Sometimes the rat thinks ‘I'm gonna win,’ but the cats just play, because if the cat just kills him instantly it's no fun, right? So, Lord Narahari actually made hardened... Hiranyakashipu think that he was going to win the battle.

Similarly when Lord Varahadev was fighting with Hiranyaksha, they were battling fighting and at one point Hiranyaksha took his club and smashed the Lord's club so hard, that the Lord's club fell out of his hand and Hiranyaksha thought ‘Haha! Just see, you, you are defeated. I am more powerful than God.’ And Varaha was simply saying, “Yes, yes you are more powerful than me.” And then a few minutes later he killed him. So, this is how Krishna deals with the demons, with the asuras: he allows them to fulfill their desires, he allows them to think for some time that they're winning, and then he destroys them. Han? So, Dhritarashtra and Duryodhana was, so keen to cheat the Pandavas, so keen to take the kingdom unfairly, that Krishna said, “All right, if that's your desire then do it, then I'll give it all to you.” How do you think the demons of today are taking over the whole earth? Han? Factually demonic forces, materialistic forces are practically the proprietors of the hub... all the great nations of the world. But how long you think it will last? Krishna says, “If you want it so bad, you're doing so many penances and austerities and cheating so bad to have it, then take it! I'll fulfill your desire, I fulfill everyone's desires for a while but in the end you have to pay your karma.” And in the end Duryodhana and Dhritarashtra paid their karma. So, you see Krishna was fulfilling the desires of them because they wanted it so bad. At the same time he was facilitated to speak of the Bhagavad-gita. At the same time he was showing the glories of devotees; how they never give up their faith and devotion under any circumstance. It's not like the common man where ‘When God does my will that I'm his devotee, but when he doesn't give me what I want then I.. then he's useless.” They were not like that. A perfect example of how a devotee is unconditionally surrendered to the Lord, was illustrated by the Pandavas in their having everything taken away. So, Krishna had many purposes to fulfill by working through Yudhishthira’s heart to convince him to gamble, and he also showed the great honor of Yudhishthira are at the cost of everything, being a king of the world his sense of honor was so high that he would never refuse a challenge. For kshatriyas of that caliber it was dishonorable for them to refuse any challenge of any sort. He was man of such integrity and honor. So those who understand Mahabharata through the through the vision of the great souls, they understand that this whole episode was simply the ultimate glorification of Yudhishthira and Krishna. But those who try to understand it with their own material senses, they find so many faults and flaws, and cannot understand due to ignorance. Do you understand now?

Yes?

Mode of goodness even though it leads to higher life is also the cause of bondage

[devotee:] Attachment is the cause of the total problem and finally everything is developing: kama, krodha, lobha, moha... The point is if somebody's attached to the... their children. Now children may not be God conscious, that is the children, they may not be Duryodhana but if they are godly people, just like here as you said sattva-guni it means attachment to the people who are even sattva-guni that is also called destruction?

[Maharaja:] It is the cause of bondage. The mode of goodness, sattva-guna leads to a higher life of material pleasure but do you not think that higher life of material pleasure does not lead to bondage? Of course! Do you think in heavenly planets people are not attached to their heavenly delights? They're more attached to, than the people on this earth. It's easier to be attached to nice things than suffering things, is it not? That's why Krishna says in Gita that there are four types of people that approach him: those who are suffering, those who are impoverished, those who are inquisitive, and those who sincerely desire the absolute truth. And Jesus says in the Bible, “It's easier to thread an elephant...a camel through the eye of a needle than, for one who was attached to the pleasures of his riches to enter into the Kingdom of God. So, the mode of goodness simply gives you material prosperity, but that material prosperity, you become very attached to it and that attachment ultimately will lead you to suffering and bondage. So, of course through the mode of goodness because there is light and enlightenment in the mode of goodness, it is very easy from that mode to take that step forward into devotion of Krishna… to Krishna. So, that is the real virtue and glory of the mode of goodness; is its easiest in that condition to transcend the modes of material nature to go one step higher and engage in devotional service. But if one, due to attachment to the pleasures of the mode of goodness is not willing to take that step forward in devotion to God, then that mode of goodness becomes the cause of his bondage. You may be attached, you may be a pious parent with pious children, but if you do not understand the needs of the soul of that child, the mode of goodness is still in the bodily concept. What about the needs of the soul of that child? The duty of a parent is to train the child, for the child never has to suffer birth and death. That means you have to take him beyond the mode of goodness – if you really love him. Does that answer your question?

Yes?

A truly God conscious person is most responsible and dutiful

[devotee:] Maharaj, you have said that we do not have to get rid of attachment, but get himself attached to the Lord, they will be detached from material. That if they are attached to God, then they will be detached from material pleasure. They don’t want to lose their relatives or son or somebody. What is actually this detachment means?

[Maharaja:] Dhruva Maharaj was completely detached, but when his brother Uttama was killed, did he just say, “Oh! very nice I'm detached.” Huh? No he retaliated and raged war on the enemy to avenge the death of his brother, but he was not simply angry because he lost his brother, he was angry because he understood the will of God: that it was his duty to defend his brother, it was duty to def... Ramachandra was thoroughly transcendental, he was God himself. But when Sita was taken away did he think ‘Oh! Who needs Sita, she's only a woman anyway’? Right? No, he was willing to wage war against the greatest Rakshasa in the world, Ravana, to protect his wife. When you become attached to Krishna, you become detached from the ignorance that is between you and your relatives. But in basis of knowledge, you are the true protector, well-wisher and lover of the soul of your relatives. That doesn't make you irresponsible, that makes you far more responsible on a higher level. You understand? Because people have a very immature sense of ignorance, to spiritual life, they think if they become God conscious they'll become so detached that they will be irresponsible in their duties in the world, but actually if a person is truly God conscious he'll become the most responsible, the most dutiful, but not on the basis of ignorance, on the basis of real knowledge.

A devotee is not sentimentally attached to family members but is their genuine well-wisher

[devotee:] Maharaj, the biggest problem for all of us is that, all the house members do not come to that thinking as such. So, we are thinking some different way, and our family members maybe thinking some other way. But, how to gain a confidence of them so that they will... we will be on the same footing?

[Maharaja:] If they're drowning, if you want to save them, you have to become an excellent swimmer just because they're drowning, it doesn't mean that you should also drown. The nature of a drowning man, is he grabs on to someone and wants to pull them down with them. That's... this… that’s just their impulsive, instinctive nature. Every lifeguard knows that; that when somebody's drowning, the drowning man the first thing he'll do is he'll try... he'll just hold on to you and try to pull you down with him with all his might. He won't be thinking he's hurting you, he'll just... that's just his nature. So, someone who's drowning due to ignorance, in the ocean of material existence the instinctive nature of that person is he wants to pull everyone who comes near him down with him. First they... in this material existence you don't even think, the people do not even think they're drowning. So, if you want to save them you should know that if you, if you're trying to save them they're gonna try to pull you down instinctively. Don't blame them for it, it's their nature. Does a lifeguard hate the drowning man? Because he's trained he knows that he's drowning ‘I feel sorry for him he's trying to pull me down but I have compassion that's just the way he's going to act in his condition.’ Right? But the lifeguard has to be a very excellent stream... swimmer and a very strong swimmer, to pull that person out. So, it's natural that if they're attached to material life, they're in the ocean of material existence, they want you to go down with them – out of ignorance. And the more you try to save them, the more you gonna... they're gonna try to pull you down. So, therefore, if you really want to help them, don't give in to the sentiments of the drowning man and say, “Oh, I'm your friend, I'll go with you, we'll drown together, very nice.” That's how a fool thinks. Some people of... so much get sentimentally attached to these drowning family members, that they think like this that ‘Oh! I don't want to hurt your feelings, so I'll go down with you.’ Are you their friend if you go down with them? You're their friend if you... whatever it takes to drag them out of the water. Right? So, the first thing you have to do is, you have to become so spiritually strong and powerful yourself that, the force of your purity will drag them out of the ocean of material existence.

Let's take the example of Prahlad; Hiranyakashipu was a very strong drowning man Hanh? And Prahlad was a little, tiny, five year old child, but the force of his purity of devotion was so strong, that his father was liberated just stopped by the force of Prahlad’s purity. Right? Hiranyakashipu attained mukti after he was killed, only because Prahlad prayed for that. Now Prahlad could have went along with his father and said, “Yes father, I'll be like you. I won't dissatisfy you.” And then Hiranyakashipu would have been very happily on the road to hell. Right? So, because Prahlad did not agree with Hiranyakashipu, Hiranyakashipu was very miserably on the road back to godhead. Prahlad made Hiranyakashipu miserable. So, the question is: if your parents are like Hiranyakashipu or your family members or your friends or your society members, are you gonna let them happily be on the road to bondage and ignorance and hell? Or you gonna give them a little bit of a miserable condition but put them on the road back to godhead? Which is love? Tell me which is a higher principle of love? Krishna says what tastes like nectar at the beginning, tastes like poison at the end. But what tastes like poison at the beginning, tastes like nectar in the end. So, your becoming a pure devotee of the Lord might taste a little like poison to them at the beginning, but in the end it would be like nectar. And your just engaging in all worldly activities just the way they want you to be might taste like nectar at the beginning but it'll be like poison for all of you in the end: whether it's in the mode of goodness, passion or ignorance, it's all poison. So, of course we want to... just like a doctor, we were discussing this afternoon; a doctor knows the child is dying and he needs an injection. Now the doctor will try to give the injection in as peaceful manner and palatable manner as possible. Did you ever see the way a child gives an injection... I mean a doctor gives an injection to a child? You know give the child some toys to play with, he'll be very gentle Aa...Ah..Aa [Maharaja makes sound], say so many sweet words telling him nice stories, nice fairy tales and he'll get his... the child's mind completely occupied in these things and then he'll say, “This won't hurt at all, this won't hurt at all, just, just, just play with your toys; this will not hurt at all.” Then he’ll give him the injection. Even though it hurts, he'll try to make the pain as little as possible. And if the child cooperates then very nice; the child is saved. But sometimes the child knows exactly what the doctor's tricks are. He's he's been through it before. And the doctor gives him the toys, “No! I don't want these toys, I know what you're gonna do.” I… And he starts fighting and scratches the doctor and tries to gouge out his eyes and bites his fingers and kicks him and swears at him and every bad word he's ever learned from his friends, he starts screaming at his doctor. Huh? What does the doctor do? Does the doctor think ‘Ah! he doesn't like me, ha ha and I'm not gonna give you the injection, I want to be your friend. Go ahead and die but if you die just know that I'm your friend; I didn't hurt you. While you're dying just know that this doctor is a good person, he didn't hurt you.’ So the doctor... No! He doesn't care if the child hates him. He knows what's good for the child. If he has to he'll grab that child and he'll just stab that dagger of a... of a syringe in his arm and give him the injection, the child will be screaming and howling and saying, “I hate you! I hate you! I want to kill you.” right? everything else. So, what is compassion? To give in to the sentiments of the child and let him die and suffer, or to go completely against the material sentiments of the child to be his well-wisher? What is love? Love is based on knowledge not ignorance. Love is based on really doing what is good for the person we love. Not... not allowing that person to destroy himself because of our sentimental attachments. That is not love.

Is there any other questions? Yes?

True satisfaction is based on self-realization

[devotee:] It is said in Bhagavatam, a person is not satisfied with whatever he has got, he will never be satisfied with whatever he gets. material world is not like that, but whatever we have got is fine but throughout life they are struggling to get more.

[Maharaja:] Material attachment which leads to lust is compared to... Bhagavad-gita to a burning fire. However much you feed a fire, is the fire ever satisfied? If you feed the fire ten thousand liters of petrol, will it say, “Ah, I don't want any more now, and now I will be peaceful”? The fire will be just that much bigger and that much ready to consume ten thousand more liters; a hundred thousand more liters. Right? The more you feed a fire, the more it wants. Lust is like that: the more you satisfy your material desires the more you want. A poor man wants to be a rich man, a rich man wants to be a millionaire, a millionaire wants to be a billionaire, a billionaire wants to be a multi-billionaire, and the multi-billionaires, they have everything possible and they still want more and they usually just go crazy: commit suicide, become drug addicts, or just become totally power-hungry to conquer the world. Nobody has ever satisfied by any amount of material acquisitions. Brahma-bhūtaḥ prasannātmā na śocati na kāṅkṣati [Bg. 18.54]. Only when one attains brahma-bhutah, the stage of self-realization, is one is satisfied – joyful: where one hankers for nothing, laments for nothing. One is self satisfied. That is real happiness. Then whether you're a poor man, or a multi-millionaire, or a multi-billionaire, you're satisfied. Doesn't mean you have to be a poor man, doesn't mean you have to be a rich man. Whatever situation in life you're in, you'll be satisfied. That doesn't mean you don't work hard for the benefit of all, but you're satisfied under all conditions. And in that satisfaction... like Arjuna, he was perfectly satisfied after hearing Bhagavad-gita, but out of duty he fought very well on battlefield of Kurukshetra. Don't think when you're satisfied you become complacent.

Srila Prabhupada ki – Jai!

Thank you very much.

[recording ends]

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
